texto: Let us love. Let our lives be a perpetual song of love for God, first of all, and for all human beings who suffer, love, and mourn. Let deep joy live in us. Let us be like the lark, enemy of the night, who always announces the dawn and awakens in each creature the love of light and life. Let us awaken others to the spiritual life.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Elizabeth LeSeur: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Let us love. Let our lives be a perpetual song of love for God, first of all, and for all human b…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que fe, darkness, light pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao-noturna e boa-noite.
